batch_summary: Deed [309] — Affidavit of Heirship and Marital History executed by John T. Black on February 15, 1958, regarding the estate of W. J. Needham (died 1952) and property in the town of Tehuacana (Block 23). This is a non-project-property instrument: JT Black served only as affiant/witness attesting to the Needham family's ownership and marital history. The Needham property (east end of south half of Block 23, bounded by South First Street and Wade Street) is not in the Black Land Project parcel inventory. Primary value: confirms JT Black alive and competent in Tehuacana 11 months before his death (1/30/1959); reveals signature discrepancy ('John R. Black' signed vs. 'John T. Black' throughout body and notarial). ISSUE-43 item 10 marked as pulled.

01_FAMILY_ENTITY_STRUCTURE.md.timestamp_summary: Deed [309] analyzed — JT Black Needham heirship affidavit (1958); signature discrepancy noted

operations.description: Update JT Black entry to note deed [309] affidavit and signature discrepancy
operations.replace_with: Pull pending. **Deed [309]**: JT Black executed an Affidavit of Heirship for the W.J. Needham estate (Tehuacana town lot, Block 23) on 2/15/1958, 11 months before his death — confirms he was alive, competent, and residing in the Tehuacana area. ⚠️ SIGNATURE DISCREPANCY: affidavit body and notarial acknowledgment both identify affiant as "John T. Black" but the signature line reads "John R. Black" — likely pen slip by a 70-year-old man, but the middle initial discrepancy is unexplained (no other instrument in the file set uses "John R. Black" for this individual). Non-project property; no Black/Bounds chain impact. |

operations.content: | [309] | John T. Black (affiant) → Public (re: W.J. Needham estate) | Limestone | ✓ | LC Pg. 466–467, Doc# 1148 (pull list cited Vol. 447 — volume not confirmed in instrument text). Filed Mar 4, 1958 at 8:00 AM; recorded Mar 5, 1958 at 4:00 PM; John Kidd, County Clerk; Lila Johnson, Deputy; Limestone County. | 466–467 (2 pages) | 1958-02-15 | 1958-03-05 | **Affidavit of Heirship and Marital History** — NON-PROJECT PROPERTY. JT Black attests to W.J. Needham family's ownership of a lot in the town of Tehuacana (east end of south half of Block 23, bounded east by South First Street, south by Wade Street) since 1921. W.J. Needham (= Will J. Needham) died 1952; married once to Mary Ellen Needham; two children: one girl (died in childhood), one boy Virgil Needham (2631 E. 35th Street, Tulsa, Oklahoma). No will, no administration. No conveyance; no Black/Bounds chain impact. ⚠️ SIGNATURE DISCREPANCY: body and notarial say "John T. Black" but signature reads "John R. Black." Notary: Mary Cogdell, Limestone County. ISSUE-43 item 10. |
